regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Tamoxifen
regimens.notes: Tamoxifen for 5 years in ER-positive DCIS treated with BCS ± RT reduces risk of ipsilateral and contralateral breast cancer recurrence. NSABP B-24 showed 3.4% absolute reduction in ipsilateral recurrence and 3.2% reduction in contralateral breast cancers with tamoxifen vs placebo at median follow-up of 13.6 years (HR 0.30 for ipsilateral, HR 0.68 for contralateral). [23]

regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Low-Dose Tamoxifen
regimens.notes: Low-dose tamoxifen (5 mg/day for 3 years) is an option if standard 20 mg dose is not tolerated. TAM-01 trial showed recurrence rate of 5.7% with tamoxifen 5 mg daily vs 11.9% with placebo (HR 0.48; 95% CI, 0.25–0.89). [61] Low-dose tamoxifen (10 mg every other day) is noted since 5-mg dose is not available in the US.

regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Aromatase Inhibitor
regimens.guideline_category: Preferred (alternative to tamoxifen in postmenopausal patients)
regimens.notes: Anastrozole demonstrated non-inferiority to tamoxifen for prevention in IBIS-II DCIS trial. NSABP B-35 showed anastrozole reduced breast cancer-free interval events vs tamoxifen (HR 0.73; 95% CI, 0.56–0.96; P = .0234) in postmenopausal patients with HR-positive DCIS treated with lumpectomy and radiation. Some advantage for AI in patients <60 years or with thromboembolism concerns. [62,63]

regimens.regimen_name: DCIS: Whole Breast RT After BCS
regimens.notes: WBRT following BCS reduces IBTR rates for all DCIS by 50%–70%. Meta-analysis of 4 trials confirmed IBTR reduction with HR 0.49 (95% CI 0.41–0.58, P < .00001). Approx half recurrences are invasive, half DCIS. Risk of local relapse increases with larger DCIS, palpable mass, grade III, margins ≤2 mm, ER-negative tumors, and age <50. Omission of RT considered only if all RTOG 9804 criteria met: screen-detected, grade 1–2, size ≤2.5 cm, margins ≥3 mm. [22-27]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: Dose-Dense AC Followed by Paclitaxel Every 2 Weeks

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles, followed by Paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV Day 1 cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles

regimens.notes: Dose-dense AC followed by paclitaxel every 2 weeks. Cycles are with myeloid growth factor support. Shows survival benefit when compared with AC followed by every-3-week paclitaxel. A 26% reduction in hazard of recurrence (P = .01) and 31% reduction in hazard of death (P = .013) for dose-dense regimens. [1,384]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: Dose-Dense AC Followed by Weekly Paclitaxel

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles, followed by Paclitaxel 80 mg/m² IV weekly for 12 weeks

regimens.notes: Weekly paclitaxel was superior to every-3-week paclitaxel in DFS (HR, 1.27; 95% CI, 1.03–1.57; P = .006) and OS (HR, 1.32; 95% CI, 1.02–1.72; P = .01). Cycles with myeloid growth factor support. [1,385]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: TC (Docetaxel/Cyclophosphamide)

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 4–6 cycles

regimens.notes: At median follow-up of 7 years, overall DFS (81% vs 75%; HR, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.56–0.98; P = .033) and OS (87% vs 82%; HR, 0.69; 95% CI, 0.50–0.97; P = .032) were significantly improved with TC compared with AC. Non-anthracycline regimen preferred for patients where anthracyclines are contraindicated. [2,386]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Olaparib for gBRCA1/2 PV
regimens.notes: 4-year iDFS was 82.7% with olaparib vs 75.4% with placebo (95% CI 3.0%–11.5%) and 4-year distant DFS was 86.5% vs 79.1% (95% CI 3.6%–11.3%). OS: 4-year OS 89.8% vs 86.4% (95% CI −0.1%–6.8%). Adjuvant olaparib can be given concurrently with endocrine therapy. [7,373]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Pembrolizumab for High-Risk TNBC (Post-Preoperative Therapy)

biomarkers.requirement: Not required for adjuvant if given in preoperative setting

regimens.notes: For high-risk patients (ypT1–4,N0 or ypN≥1) with pembrolizumab given in preoperative setting. 5-year EFS: 81.3% with chemo+pembrolizumab vs 72.3% with placebo (HR 0.63; 95% CI 0.49-0.81). Among those with pCR who received adjuvant pembrolizumab, 5-year EFS was 92.2% vs 88.2% with chemo alone. [273,274]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Capecitabine for Residual Disease After Preoperative Chemotherapy
regimens.site: Breast, invasive, HR-/HER2- (TNBC) with residual disease

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–14, cycled every 21 days for 6–8 cycles

regimens.notes: CREATE-X evaluated adjuvant capecitabine in patients with residual invasive disease after standard preoperative chemotherapy. Improved DFS (HR 0.70, 95% CI 0.53-0.92, P = .01) and OS (HR for death 0.59, 95% CI 0.39-0.90, P = .01). OS was higher in TNBC (HR for death 0.52). [6,215]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Capecitabine Maintenance Therapy for TNBC

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days for 1 year

regimens.notes: Maintenance low-dose capecitabine improved 5-year DFS in TNBC: 85.8% vs 75.8% (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.38-0.92; P = .02). 5-year OS 85.5% vs 81.3% (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.47-1.19; P = .22). [398]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant TCHP (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles

drugs.name: Pertuzumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles

regimens.notes: TCHP is a preferred regimen for HER2-positive disease. High pCR rates with pertuzumab-containing regimens (57%–66% in TRYPHAENA). After surgery, complete up to 1 year of HER2-targeted therapy. [28,270,403]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant AC Followed by TCH (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg loading then 2 mg/kg weekly (or 6 mg/kg every 21 days)
drugs.schedule: Cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles, followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year

regimens.notes: BCIRG 006 showed AC-TH and TCH were both superior to AC-T without trastuzumab. TCH had significantly lower cardiac toxicity (9.4% vs 18.6% LVEF decline, P < .0001; CHF 0.4% vs 2%, P < .001). TCH preferred for patients with cardiac risk factors. [27,403]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab (TCH-free)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: To complete 1 year of treatment (or 6 mg/kg every 21 days as alternative)

regimens.notes: Preferred for low-risk T1,N0,M0 HER2-positive disease, particularly those not eligible for other standard adjuvant regimens due to comorbidities. 3-year DFS was 98.7% (95% CI 97.6–99.8). 10-year invasive DFS 97.0% (95% CI 95.2-98.7%), 10-year breast cancer-specific survival 98.8%, 10-year OS 94.3%. Heart failure rate 0.5%. [26,404,405]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab + Chemotherapy (APHINITY)

drugs.name: Pertuzumab
drugs.schedule: With chemotherapy then maintenance with trastuzumab to complete 1 year

regimens.notes: Updated results with median follow-up of 11.3 years confirmed benefit of adding pertuzumab to trastuzumab plus chemotherapy in node-positive disease: 8-year invasive DFS 86% vs 81% (HR 0.72; 95% CI 0.60-0.87). No benefit seen in node-negative subgroup. [408,409]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for Residual Disease (HER2-Positive)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease

drugs.name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 14 cycles

regimens.notes: For those with high risk of recurrence: defined as inoperable cancer (cT4, N0–3, M0 or cT1–3, N2–3, M0) at presentation or operable cancer (cT1–3, N0–1, M0) with axillary node–positive disease (ypN1–3) following preoperative therapy. Associated with ILD/pneumonitis risk; regular monitoring recommended. [30]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Ado-Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1) for Residual Disease (HER2-Positive)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease

drugs.name: Ado-trastuzumab emtansine
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 14 cycles

regimens.notes: KATHERINE trial: 3-year invasive DFS 88.3% with T-DM1 vs 77.0% with trastuzumab (HR 0.50, 95% CI 0.39-0.64, P < .001). If T-DM1 discontinued for toxicity, complete up to 1 year of HER2-directed therapy with trastuzumab ± pertuzumab. [410]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Abemaciclib + Endocrine Therapy

drugs.name: Abemaciclib
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days for 2 years

regimens.notes: 2 years of adjuvant abemaciclib combined with endocrine therapy. For high-risk breast cancer defined as: ≥4 positive lymph nodes confirmed preoperatively and/or at surgery, or 1–3 positive lymph nodes with either grade 3 disease or tumor size ≥5 cm. Reduced absolute risk of recurrence at 4 years by 6.4% (HR 0.664, 95% CI 0.578-0.762, P < .0001). VTE risk should be considered when combining with tamoxifen. [369]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Ribociclib + Endocrine Therapy

drugs.name: Ribociclib
drugs.schedule: Once daily on Days 1–21, cycled every 28 days for 3 years

regimens.notes: For patients with any lymph node involvement (excluding microscopic nodal involvement), or if no nodal involvement, either tumor size >5 cm, or tumor size 2–5 cm either grade 2 (and high genomic risk or Ki-67 ≥20%) or grade 3. Invasive DFS: 3.3% improvement (HR 0.75, 95% CI 0.62-0.91, P = .003). [4,372]

regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Ribociclib)
regimens.notes: MONALEESA-2: PFS 25.3 vs 16.0 months (HR 0.56; 95% CI 0.45–0.70). OS: 63.9 vs 51.4 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.63–0.93). MONALEESA-7 (pre/peri-menopausal): PFS 23.8 vs 13.0 months. OS: 58.7 vs 48.0 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.61–0.96). QTcF monitoring required. [499,501,502,503]

regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Abemaciclib)
regimens.notes: PFS: median not reached vs 14.7 months (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.41–0.72). ORR 59% vs 44%. Final OS analysis: median 66.8 vs 53.7 months (HR 0.80; 95% CI 0.64–1.02), no statistical significance. ILD/pneumonitis reported in 5.2%. [504,505]

regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Palbociclib)
regimens.notes: PFS 24.8 vs 14.5 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.46–0.72). Final OS (90.1-month median follow-up) showed no statistically significant difference: 53.9 vs 51.2 months (HR 0.96; 95% CI 0.78–1.2). Neutropenia 66.5%. [506,507]

regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant/Inavolisib/Palbociclib for PIK3CA-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (progression on adjuvant ET or relapse within 12 months)

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

regimens.notes: PFS: 15.0 vs 7.3 months (HR 0.43; 95% CI 0.32–0.59). Median OS: 34 vs 27 months (HR 0.67; 95% CI 0.48–0.94). Higher rates of neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, hyperglycemia, and stomatitis with inavolisib. [510,511]

regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Ribociclib) for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second-line or after progression on adjuvant ET

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

regimens.notes: PFS: 21 vs 13 months (HR 0.59; 95% CI 0.48–0.73). At 42 months: estimated OS 57.8% (95% CI 52.0–63.2) vs 45.9% (95% CI 36.9–54.5). OS benefit demonstrated. [512,513]

regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Abemaciclib) for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second-line or after progression on ET

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + Alpelisib for PIK3CA-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

regimens.notes: PFS: 11.0 months (95% CI 7.5–14.5) with alpelisib vs 5.7 months (95% CI 3.7–7.4) with fulvestrant alone (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.50–0.85; P < .001). Hyperglycemia 36.6% grade 3/4. [519]

regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + Capivasertib for PI3K Pathway-Altered HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

biomarkers.requirement: PIK3CA or AKT1 activating mutations or PTEN inactivating mutations and genomic changes

regimens.notes: Overall PFS: 7.2 vs 3.6 months (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.51–0.71). In PI3K pathway-altered: 7.3 vs 3.1 months (HR 0.50; 95% CI 0.38–0.65). Grade ≥3 adverse events: rash 12.1%, diarrhea 9.3%. Hyperglycemia any grade 16.3%. FDA-approved for PIK3CA, AKT1, or PTEN alterations. [520]

regimens.regimen_name: Elacestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

drugs.name: Elacestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

regimens.notes: In overall population, PFS: 2.79 vs 1.9 months (HR 0.69; P = .0018). In subgroup with prior ET + CDK4/6 inhibitor ≥12 months, median PFS 8.6 vs 1.9 months (HR 0.41; 95% CI 0.26–0.63). 72% had ESR1-mutated tumors. [528,529]

regimens.regimen_name: Imlunestrant + Abemaciclib for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

biomarkers.requirement: ESR1 mutation (most effective in ESR1-mutated, no prior CDK4/6 inhibitor)

regimens.notes: Combination PFS: 9.4 vs 5.5 months (HR 0.57; 95% CI 0.44–0.73) vs imlunestrant alone. ESR1-mutated subgroup: median PFS 11.1 vs 5.5 months (HR 0.53; 95% CI 0.35–0.80). Single-agent imlunestrant improved PFS 5.5 vs 3.8 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.44-0.82) in ESR1-mutated. Grade ≥3 toxicities: 49% vs 17% with combination vs single agent. Most effective in ESR1 mutations and no prior CDK4/6 inhibitor. [6,530]

regimens.regimen_name: Vepdegestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.setting: After prior endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor in metastatic setting

drugs.name: Vepdegestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

regimens.notes: ESR1-mutated HR+/HER2- subgroup: PFS 5.0 vs 2.1 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.43–0.78; P < .001). Overall population: PFS 3.8 vs 3.6 months (HR 0.83; 95% CI 0.69–1.01; P = .07), did not meet primary endpoint. Grade ≥3 AEs: 23.4% vs 17.6%. [18]

regimens.regimen_name: Everolimus + Exemestane for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
regimens.notes: PFS: 11.0 vs 4.1 months (HR 0.38; 95% CI 0.31–0.48; P < .0001). Must meet BOLERO-2 criteria (progression within 12 months on nonsteroidal AI). If progression on everolimus, no data for additional everolimus regimen. Tamoxifen or fulvestrant + everolimus are also options. [523,524]

regimens.regimen_name: Docetaxel + Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)
regimens.notes: PFS: 18.5 vs 12.4 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.51–0.75; P < .001). OS: 34% reduction in death risk at 30 months (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.52–0.84; P = .0008). Followed by maintenance pertuzumab + trastuzumab. If HR-positive: add endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib). [36,564,565]

regimens.regimen_name: Paclitaxel + Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)

drugs.name: Paclitaxel
drugs.dose: 80 mg/m² weekly or 175 mg/m² every 21 days

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg loading then 2 mg/kg weekly or 8 mg/kg then 6 mg/kg every 21 days

regimens.notes: PERUSE study: median PFS comparable between docetaxel, paclitaxel, and nab-paclitaxel (19.6, 23.0, and 18.1 months). If HR-positive, add endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib) with trastuzumab ± pertuzumab as maintenance. [37,38,39,568]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki + Pertuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)
regimens.notes: PFS: 40.7 vs 26.9 months (HR 0.46; 95% CI 0.44–0.71) vs THP. ORR 85% vs 79%. ILD/pneumonitis 12% (vs 1% THP), fatal AEs 3.4% vs 0.8%. Not preferred first-line for most patients due to ILD risk; considered for select patients including those with CNS involvement. [27,572]

regimens.regimen_name: Capecitabine/Tucatinib + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic
regimens.setting: Second/third-line and beyond metastatic (especially with CNS progression)
regimens.notes: Preferred for both systemic and CNS progression. 1-year PFS rate: 33% vs 12% (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.42–0.71); median PFS 7.8 vs 5.6 months. 2-year OS: 45% vs 27% (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.50–0.88). In patients with brain metastases, 1-year PFS 25% vs 0%. May also be given in second-line. [39,573]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki (T-DXd) for HER2+ Metastatic (Second/Third Line)
regimens.notes: DESTINY-Breast02: PFS 17.8 vs 6.9 months (HR 0.36; 95% CI 0.28–0.45); OS 39.2 vs 26.5 months (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.50–0.86). May also be used in first-line for select patients with rapid progression within 6 months of neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy. ILD/pneumonitis risk. [41,573,574]

regimens.regimen_name: Ado-Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1) for HER2+ Metastatic (Second Line)
regimens.notes: EMILIA: PFS 9.6 vs 6.4 months (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.55–0.77; P < .001); significant OS improvement (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.48–0.81; P = .0005). If not a candidate for fam-trastuzumab, T-DM1 may be used second-line. [40,576]

regimens.regimen_name: Capecitabine + Neratinib for HER2+ Metastatic

drugs.name: Neratinib
drugs.dose: 120 mg Days 1–7 then 160 mg Days 8–14 then 240 mg Days 15–21 (cycle 1); then 240 mg daily Days 1–21 from cycle 2

regimens.notes: NALA: Risk of progression reduced by 24% (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.63–0.93; P = .0059). Fewer patients required CNS intervention. ORR 32.8% vs 26.7% (P = .1201). Diarrhea 83% any grade (24% grade 3/4). [55,589]

regimens.regimen_name: Chemotherapy + Margetuximab for HER2+ Metastatic (Fourth Line+)
regimens.notes: PFS: 5.8 vs 4.9 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.59–0.98). OS: 21.6 vs 21.9 months. Serious AEs comparable between groups. [56,590,591]

regimens.regimen_name: Chemotherapy + Pembrolizumab for TNBC (PD-L1 CPS ≥10, First-Line)
Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy + Pembrolizumab for TNBC (First-Line)
regimens.notes: PFS: 11.2 vs 7.8 months with chemo+pembrolizumab (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.51–0.84). ORR 60% vs 53%. No new safety concerns with combination. [25,599]

regimens.regimen_name: Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy Monotherapy for TNBC (First-Line, CPS <10)
regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1)

biomarkers.requirement: CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor

regimens.notes: PFS: 9.7 vs 6.9 months with chemotherapy (HR 0.62; P < .001). Response duration 12.2 vs 7.2 months. PFS2: 18.2 vs 14 months (HR 0.70; 95% CI 0.55–0.90) despite crossover, supporting upfront use. [600]

regimens.regimen_name: Datopotamab Deruxtecan-dlnk for TNBC (First-Line, CPS <10)
regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1)

biomarkers.requirement: CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor

regimens.notes: PFS: 10.8 vs 5.6 months (HR 0.57; P < .0001). OS: 23.7 vs 18.7 months (HR 0.79; P = .029). No crossover in trial. Toxicity ≥ grade 3: 33% vs 29%. [15,563]

regimens.regimen_name: PARP Inhibitor (Olaparib or Talazoparib) for gBRCA1/2 TNBC
regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 and gBRCA1/2 PV)
regimens.notes: OlympiAD (olaparib vs chemo): PFS 7.0 vs 4.2 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.43–0.80; P < .001). EMBRACA (talazoparib vs chemo): PFS 8.6 vs 5.6 months (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.41–0.71; P < .001). Available evidence suggests more effective earlier. Also indicated for platinum-sensitive TNBC. [9,17,643,645]

regimens.regimen_name: Platinum Monotherapy for gBRCA1/2 TNBC
regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 and gBRCA1/2 PV)
regimens.notes: TNT trial: In gBRCA1/2 mutant subgroup, ORR to carboplatin 68.0% vs docetaxel 33.3% (P = .03). PFS also improved with carboplatin. Somatic BRCA1/2 mutation carriers did not show the same advantage. [12,13,646]

regimens.regimen_name: Datopotamab Deruxtecan-dlnk for HR+/HER2- Metastatic (Second-Line+)
regimens.notes: PFS benefit observed; 9-month PFS rates approximately double vs chemotherapy (37.5% vs 18.7%; HR 0.63). OS not statistically significant (HR 1.01; 95% CI 0.83-1.22; P = .9445). Not recommended after prior ADC. Benefit of use in patients with prior ADC treatment not known. [15,562,563]

regimens.regimen_name: Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy for HR+/HER2- Metastatic (Second-Line+)
regimens.notes: After prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitor, and at least 2 lines of chemotherapy. OS: 14.4 vs 11.2 months (HR 0.79; 95% CI 0.65–0.96; P = .020). ORR 21% vs 14%. Higher neutropenia, diarrhea, nausea, alopecia. [24,561]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HR+/HER2-Low or HER2-Ultralow Metastatic (After Endocrine Therapy)
regimens.site: Breast, invasive, HR+/HER2-low (IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-) or HER2-ultralow (IHC 0+)

biomarkers.requirement: HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH negative (HER2-low) or IHC 0+ with membrane staining (HER2-ultralow)

regimens.notes: HER2-low subgroup: PFS 13.2 vs 8.1 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.51–0.71) vs capecitabine or taxane chemotherapy. HER2-ultralow: PFS 13.2 vs 8.3 months (HR 0.78; 95% CI 0.50–1.21). OS differences not yet statistically significant. ILD/pneumonitis 11% (0.7% fatal). Not first-line for most patients with de novo metastatic disease given available alternatives. [560]

regimens.regimen_name: Pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR Metastatic Breast Cancer
regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.dose: 200 mg IV or 2 mg/kg every 21 days or 400 mg every 6 weeks
drugs.schedule: Until progression or up to 24 months

biomarkers.requirement: Microsatellite instability-high or mismatch repair deficient

regimens.notes: FDA-approved for MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb) solid tumors after prior treatment with no satisfactory alternatives. [10,11,653,654]

regimens.regimen_name: Larotrectinib/Entrectinib/Repotrectinib for NTRK Fusion-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg daily Days 1–14 then 160 mg twice daily (cycle 1); then 160 mg twice daily continuously

biomarkers.requirement: NTRK gene fusion without acquired resistance mutation

regimens.notes: FDA-approved for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors without acquired resistance mutation or after progression following treatment. Repotrectinib may be used first-line (category 2B). [8,15,650,651,652]

regimens.regimen_name: Selpercatinib for RET Fusion-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives
regimens.notes: FDA-approved for RET fusion-positive solid tumors after prior systemic treatment or no satisfactory alternatives. [16,656,657]

regimens.regimen_name: Dostarlimab-gxly for MSI-H/dMMR Metastatic Solid Tumors
regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.dose: 500 mg IV Days 1, cycles 1–4; then 1000 mg IV Day 1 every 42 days from cycle 5
drugs.schedule: Every 21 days for cycles 1–4 then every 42 days

biomarkers.requirement: Microsatellite instability-high or mismatch repair deficient

regimens.notes: ORR 44% in dMMR/MSI-H tumors, complete response 13%. Median PFS 6.9 months, OS not reached. FDA-approved for adult patients with MSI-H/dMMR unresectable or metastatic tumors that progressed on/following prior treatment. [3,655]

regimens.regimen_name: Inflammatory Breast Cancer: Preoperative Anthracycline-Based Chemotherapy
regimens.notes: NCCN Panel recommends preoperative systemic therapy with anthracycline-based regimen with or without taxanes for initial treatment of IBC. Complete planned chemotherapy prior to mastectomy. If not completed preoperatively, complete postoperatively. If HER2-positive, add trastuzumab for up to 1 year. [732,733,734]

regimens.regimen_name: Inflammatory Breast Cancer: Preoperative Chemotherapy + Trastuzumab (HER2+)
regimens.site: Breast, invasive, inflammatory breast cancer, HER2-positive

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with chemotherapy, continue up to 1 year total

regimens.notes: NOAH trial showed addition of trastuzumab to neoadjuvant chemotherapy significantly improved response rate and event-free survival in HER2-positive locally advanced/IBC. Trastuzumab continued for up to 1 year. A pertuzumab-containing regimen may be administered preoperatively for HER2-positive IBC (per Neosphere data). [414,743]

regimens.regimen_name: IBC: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy (Post-Surgery, HR-Positive)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant after mastectomy and chemotherapy completion

drugs.name: Endocrine therapy
drugs.schedule: Per standard schedule (tamoxifen 5 years or AI 5 years)

regimens.notes: All patients with HR-positive IBC are recommended to receive endocrine therapy sequentially after completing the planned preoperative systemic therapy. Endocrine therapy options follow standard HR+/HER2-negative guidelines. [733]

regimens.regimen_name: Bone Metastases: Denosumab, Zoledronic Acid, or Pamidronate
regimens.site: Breast, invasive, any subtype with bone metastases
regimens.notes: Given in addition to systemic therapy if bone metastasis present, expected survival ≥3 months, and renal function adequate. Optimal zoledronic acid dosing every 12 weeks (ZOOM, CALGB 70604, OPTIMIZE-2 trials). Calcium and vitamin D supplementation required. Dental examination with preventive dentistry recommended prior to therapy. Denosumab superior to zoledronic acid for time to first SRE (HR 0.82; 95% CI 0.71–0.95; P = .01 for superiority). [487,488,489,492]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy: AI for 5 Years (Postmenopausal)
regimens.notes: ATAC: fewer recurrences with anastrozole vs tamoxifen (HR 0.85; 95% CI 0.76–0.94; P = .003). BIG 1-98: superior DFS with letrozole vs tamoxifen (HR 0.81; 95% CI 0.70–0.93; P = .003). Three AIs have similar efficacy and toxicity. Consider extended therapy 7.5–10 years for high-risk node-positive patients. Baseline BMD recommended. [296,297,298,304,307]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy: Tamoxifen 5 Years (Premenopausal or Postmenopausal)

drugs.name: Tamoxifen
drugs.schedule: Daily for 5 years (consider extending to 10 years)

regimens.notes: EBCTCG overview: tamoxifen decreases annual odds of recurrence by 41% and death by 31%. ATLAS: extending to 10 years reduces recurrence (HR 0.75 during years 10–14) and mortality. aTTom confirmed significant reduction in recurrence and death with 10 vs 5 years. [59,289,290,291,292]

regimens.regimen_name: OFS + Exemestane (Premenopausal High-Risk HR+ Breast Cancer)

drugs.name: GnRH agonist (goserelin, leuprolide, or triptorelin)
drugs.dose: Goserelin 3.6 mg SC q4wk or 10.8 mg SC q12wk; Leuprolide 3.75–7.5 mg IM/SC q4wk or 11.25–22.5 mg q12wk; Triptorelin 3.75 mg IM q4wk or 11.25 mg IM q12wk

regimens.notes: DFS 92.8% with OFS+exemestane vs 88.8% with OFS+tamoxifen (HR 0.66; P < .001). 15-year DFS 73.5% (OFS+exemestane) vs 70.5% (OFS+tamoxifen) vs 67.0% (tamoxifen alone). Considered for premenopausal patients with high-risk features: young age/longevity, high-grade tumor, lymph node involvement. 5 years optimal duration. [314,315,316]

regimens.regimen_name: Cytotoxic Chemotherapy Regimens for HR+/HER2- or HR-/HER2- Metastatic (Preferred Single Agents)

drugs.name: Paclitaxel
drugs.dose: 175 mg/m² IV Day 1 or 80 mg/m² IV weekly

drugs.name: Vinorelbine
drugs.dose: 25 mg/m² IV Day 1 weekly or 20–35 mg/m² Days 1 and 8 every 21 days

regimens.notes: Sequential single agents preferred. Combination therapy only for rapid progression, high tumor burden, or visceral crisis. NCCN notes the selection/dosing/management is complex and requires experienced team. Cytotoxic therapy includes chemotherapy, ADCs, and targeted therapy. [BINV-Q 5 of 15]

regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin for TNBC Metastatic
regimens.notes: Listed as other recommended regimen for HR+/HER2- and HR-/HER2- metastatic breast cancer. Platinum agents have demonstrated activity in metastatic TNBC. [BINV-Q 5 of 15]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HER2-Low Metastatic (Second-Line After Prior Chemotherapy)
regimens.setting: Second-line metastatic after 1-2 prior chemotherapy regimens
regimens.notes: PFS: 9.9 vs 5.1 months (HR 0.50). OS: 23 vs 17 months (HR 0.69). HR+ subset: PFS 10.1 vs 5.4 months (HR 0.51; 95% CI 0.40–0.64), OS 23.9 vs 17.6 months (HR 0.69; 95% CI 0.55–0.87). ILD/pneumonitis 12%, fatal 0.8%. [26,560]

regimens.regimen_name: Imlunestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic

drugs.name: Imlunestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

regimens.notes: Single-agent imlunestrant improved median PFS 5.5 vs 3.8 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.44-0.82, P < .001) among ESR1-mutated patients; benefit not seen in overall population. [6,530]

regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy + HER2-Targeted Therapy for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (Maintenance After THP)
regimens.setting: Maintenance after first-line chemo + THP in metastatic
regimens.notes: PATINA: median PFS 44 months with palbociclib vs 29 months without (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.59–0.96). After taxane-based induction with THP, maintenance endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib) may be added to maintenance trastuzumab ± pertuzumab. Also supported by PERTAIN trial data. [571,596]

regimens.regimen_name: Abemaciclib + Fulvestrant + Trastuzumab for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (Fourth Line+)
regimens.notes: monarcHER: PFS 8.3 months with triple therapy vs 5.7 months (HR 0.67; 95% CI 0.45–1.0) vs trastuzumab + chemotherapy. ORR 33% vs 14%. Data immature for OS. [78]

regimens.regimen_name: Preoperative Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki Followed by THP for HER2-Positive Early Breast Cancer
regimens.setting: Preoperative for high-risk early breast cancer

drugs.name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 4 cycles

regimens.notes: For HER2-positive breast cancer with high-risk of recurrence including lymph node positive (N1-3) disease or primary tumor stage T3-4 or inflammatory early breast cancer. Excludes patients with ILD/pneumonitis requiring steroids, ILD/pneumonitis at screening, or ECOG PS >1. [42]

regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy for HR+/HER2- Preoperative (Aromatase Inhibitor ± OFS)
regimens.guideline_category: Preferred (for selected patients with comorbidities or low-risk luminal biology)
regimens.notes: Preoperative endocrine therapy may be considered for patients with comorbidities or low-risk luminal biology based on clinical characteristics/genomic signatures. Optimal duration 4–6 months if response achieved. TransNEOS data showed correlation between 21-gene assay Recurrence Score and clinical response to preoperative letrozole. [258,260,268]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine + Olaparib for gBRCA1/2 PV After Preoperative Therapy (HR+/HER2-)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease or ≥4 positive lymph nodes
regimens.notes: Adjuvant olaparib may be given concurrently with endocrine therapy after completion of RT. For those with ≥4 positive lymph nodes after adjuvant chemotherapy or residual disease after preoperative therapy. In patients eligible for both olaparib and abemaciclib/ribociclib, the optimal sequence is not known. [7,373]

regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy + CDK4/6 Inhibitor + Trastuzumab ± Pertuzumab for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line Alternative)
regimens.notes: PERTAIN: PFS 18.9 vs 15.8 months (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.48–0.89) for pertuzumab+trastuzumab+AI vs trastuzumab+AI. Less toxic than chemotherapy-based options. TAnDEM: PFS 4.8 vs 2.4 months for AI+trastuzumab vs AI alone. Premenopausal patients require OFS. [592,596]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HER2-Low Metastatic (After Prior Chemotherapy, Second-Line)
regimens.notes: For those with HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH negative after 1-2 prior chemotherapy regimens. Based on DESTINY-Breast04. For HER2 IHC 0+, category 2A based on DESTINY-Breast06 (not yet showing OS benefit). [26,560]

regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Neratinib After Trastuzumab for HR+/HER2+ High-Risk
regimens.notes: ExteNET: improved invasive DFS with neratinib after 1 year adjuvant trastuzumab (HR 0.73; 95% CI 0.57–0.92; P = .0083), primarily in HR-positive subgroup (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.43–0.83; P = .063). Benefit/toxicities with neratinib after pertuzumab or ado-trastuzumab emtansine unknown. [i,39]

regimens.regimen_name: Preoperative Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab followed by AC/Epirubicin + Pembrolizumab followed by Adjuvant Pembrolizumab for TNBC
regimens.notes: KEYNOTE-522: preoperative carboplatin/paclitaxel + pembrolizumab followed by AC/Epirubicin + cyclophosphamide + pembrolizumab followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab. Cycles: 4 preoperative chemo cycles, then 4 adjuvant chemo cycles, then 9 cycles adjuvant pembrolizumab. Pembrolizumab can also be given at 400 mg or 4 mg/kg every 42 days. [5,273,274]
